|Name = Kunigami Shrine |Image = [[File:Kunigami Shrine, Maruoka.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879|1=Fukui Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakai City|Q990342|WD=Q990342|1=Sakai, Fukui}}{{ill|lt=Maruoka Town|Q177273|WD=Q177273|1=Maruoka, Fukui}} Ishikido-machi 1-2 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Prince Maruko |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = Before 1200 |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Annual festivals = {{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}}, {{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963|1=October 16}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukui|label=Kunigami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kunigami Shrine''' (くにがみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Maruoka Town, {{ill|lt=Sakai City|Q990342|WD=Q990342|1=Sakai, Fukui}}, {{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879|1=Fukui Prefecture}}. Its former shrine rank was prefectural shrine. It enshrines Prince Maruko, a son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|WD=Q349277|1=Emperor Keitai}}. ===History=== Listed as “Kunigami Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Sakai District|Q7402764|WD=Q7402764|1=Sakai District, Fukui}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} founded before 1200Information board within the shrine grounds. According to the shrine tradition recorded in the *Shrine Register*, the shrine originated when the placenta of Prince Maruko, the second son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|WD=Q349277|1=Emperor Keitai}} and Yahime, was buried at the place where the prince was born, called “Maruoko” (磨留古乎加), and a Shinmei shrine was established there[http://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20151027_104515 Fukui Prefecture Shrine Association]. In {{ill|lt=1576|Q6700|WD=Q6700|1=1576}} (Tenshō 4), when {{ill|lt=Shibata Katsutoyo|Q865901|WD=Q865901|1=Shibata Katsutoyo}} built {{ill|lt=Maruoka Castle|Q1143892|WD=Q1143892|1=Maruoka Castle}} at “Maruoko,” the shrine was relocated from its original site to its present location. Thereafter, it received protection and donations from successive lords of Maruoka Castle. On November 18, {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} (Meiji 7), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and on April 26, {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}}, it was specified as a shrine to receive offerings of sacred coins and ritual sticks. The {{ill|lt=1948|Q5165|WD=Q5165|1=1948}} (Shōwa 23) {{ill|lt=Fukui earthquake|Q4389555|WD=Q4389555|lt=Great Fukui Earthquake|1=1948 Fukui earthquake}} on June 28 caused the shrine buildings and trees to collapse and burn, but on July 15, {{ill|lt=1962|Q2764|WD=Q2764|1=1962}}, the main hall (Shinmei-zukuri), offering hall, and worship hall were rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1982|Q2436|WD=Q2436|1=1982}}, as a commemorative project marking the 20th anniversary of reconstruction, a treasure hall was newly built and a sacred fence erected. ===Cultural Properties=== Among its many cultural assets are the “Hanging Scroll of the Hakusan Pilgrimage Mandala, color on silk,” dating back to the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} (designated a cultural property of Fukui Prefecture{{Cite web|ja|author= |year= |url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/kaiga/itiran.html |title=Fukui Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties (Hanging Scroll of the Hakusan Pilgrimage Mandala, color on silk) |publisher=Fukui Prefecture |accessdate=2018-02-16}}), as well as the “Heavenly Treasure Sword,” said to have been dedicated by {{ill|lt=Honda Shigeaki|Q10517896|WD=Q10517896|1=Honda Shigeaki}}, lord of Maruoka Castle, and the Senju Kannon of Jambudvīpa gold. Within the shrine grounds is also a stone lantern dedicated by {{ill|lt=Arima Michizumi|Q10517531|WD=Q10517531|1=Arima Michizumi}}, the final lord of the {{ill|lt=Maruoka Domain|Q10863481|WD=Q10863481|1=Maruoka Domain}} who also served as a shogunate {{ill|lt=Rōjū|Q1196812|WD=Q1196812|1=Rōjū}}. ===Treasures=== * '''Hanging Scroll of Hakusan Toyohara Mandala, color on silk''' : Said to have been produced in the Muromachi period, it is regarded as an important historical artifact of {{ill|lt=Hakusan worship|Q11579557|WD=Q11579557|1=Hakusan Faith}}{{Cite book|ja|author=Iwasaki Shinnosuke |title=Folk Tales and Legends of Echizen Maruoka |publisher=Iwasaki Shinnosuke |year=1979 |page=27–28 |isbn=}}. * '''Heavenly Treasure Sword''' : A treasured sword dedicated by Honda Shigeaki, lord of Maruoka. The scabbard features {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=inlay work|1=UNKNOWN}}, and the {{ill|lt=tsuba|Q1758043|WD=Q1758043|1=tsuba}} (handguard) is made of gold. The scabbard is said to be decorated with *kan-shippō*. As of 2019 (Reiwa 1), it is preserved as a treasure of Kunigami Shrine. * '''Senju Kannon of Jambudvīpa Gold''' : In {{ill|lt=862|Q24993|WD=Q24993|1=862}} (Jōgan 4), it was brought back from {{ill|lt=Tang China|Q9683|WD=Q9683|1=Tang dynasty}} by the monk Shinnyo of Settsu and is housed in a {{ill|lt=zushi|Q19993032|WD=Q19993032|1=Zushi}} made of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=ebony|1=UNKNOWN}}. Seven ring-like ornaments hang from the halo of the Kannon, with their bases made of gold, silver, and {{ill|lt=shakudō|Q11635994|WD=Q11635994|1=shakudō}}, inlaid with red, blue, and white jewels. The zushi is carved with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lotus flowers|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its curtain is adorned with beautiful gold and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lapis lazuli|1=UNKNOWN}}. ===Footnotes=== {{Reflist}} ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Kunigami Shrine}} * [http://www.kunigamijinja.jp/ Kunigami Shrine] – Official website == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kunigami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kunigami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echizen Province]] [[Category:坂井市の建築物]] [[Category:坂井市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:福井県の旧県社]] [[Category:越前国の式内社]] [[Category:丸岡藩]] [[Category:Sankei Mandalas]] [[Category:pages]]

|Name = Kunigami Shrine |Image = [[File:Kunigami Shrine, Maruoka.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879|1=Fukui Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakai City|Q990342|WD=Q990342|1=Sakai, Fukui}}{{ill|lt=Maruoka Town|Q177273|WD=Q177273|1=Maruoka, Fukui}} Ishikido-machi 1-2 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Prince Maruko |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = Before 1200 |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Annual festivals = {{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}}, {{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963|1=October 16}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukui|label=Kunigami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kunigami Shrine''' (くにがみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Maruoka Town, {{ill|lt=Sakai City|Q990342|WD=Q990342|1=Sakai, Fukui}}, {{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879|1=Fukui Prefecture}}. Its former shrine rank was prefectural shrine. It enshrines Prince Maruko, a son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|WD=Q349277|1=Emperor Keitai}}. ===History=== Listed as “Kunigami Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Sakai District|Q7402764|WD=Q7402764|1=Sakai District, Fukui}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} founded before 1200Information board within the shrine grounds. According to the shrine tradition recorded in the *Shrine Register*, the shrine originated when the placenta of Prince Maruko, the second son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|WD=Q349277|1=Emperor Keitai}} and Yahime, was buried at the place where the prince was born, called “Maruoko” (磨留古乎加), and a Shinmei shrine was established there[http://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20151027_104515 Fukui Prefecture Shrine Association]. In {{ill|lt=1576|Q6700|WD=Q6700|1=1576}} (Tenshō 4), when {{ill|lt=Shibata Katsutoyo|Q865901|WD=Q865901|1=Shibata Katsutoyo}} built {{ill|lt=Maruoka Castle|Q1143892|WD=Q1143892|1=Maruoka Castle}} at “Maruoko,” the shrine was relocated from its original site to its present location. Thereafter, it received protection and donations from successive lords of Maruoka Castle. On November 18, {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} (Meiji 7), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and on April 26, {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}}, it was specified as a shrine to receive offerings of sacred coins and ritual sticks. The {{ill|lt=1948|Q5165|WD=Q5165|1=1948}} (Shōwa 23) {{ill|lt=Fukui earthquake|Q4389555|WD=Q4389555|lt=Great Fukui Earthquake|1=1948 Fukui earthquake}} on June 28 caused the shrine buildings and trees to collapse and burn, but on July 15, {{ill|lt=1962|Q2764|WD=Q2764|1=1962}}, the main hall (Shinmei-zukuri), offering hall, and worship hall were rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1982|Q2436|WD=Q2436|1=1982}}, as a commemorative project marking the 20th anniversary of reconstruction, a treasure hall was newly built and a sacred fence erected. ===Cultural Properties=== Among its many cultural assets are the “Hanging Scroll of the Hakusan Pilgrimage Mandala, color on silk,” dating back to the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} (designated a cultural property of Fukui Prefecture{{Cite web|ja|author= |year= |url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/kaiga/itiran.html |title=Fukui Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties (Hanging Scroll of the Hakusan Pilgrimage Mandala, color on silk) |publisher=Fukui Prefecture |accessdate=2018-02-16}}), as well as the “Heavenly Treasure Sword,” said to have been dedicated by {{ill|lt=Honda Shigeaki|Q10517896|WD=Q10517896|1=Honda Shigeaki}}, lord of Maruoka Castle, and the Senju Kannon of Jambudvīpa gold. Within the shrine grounds is also a stone lantern dedicated by {{ill|lt=Arima Michizumi|Q10517531|WD=Q10517531|1=Arima Michizumi}}, the final lord of the {{ill|lt=Maruoka Domain|Q10863481|WD=Q10863481|1=Maruoka Domain}} who also served as a shogunate {{ill|lt=Rōjū|Q1196812|WD=Q1196812|1=Rōjū}}. ===Treasures=== * '''Hanging Scroll of Hakusan Toyohara Mandala, color on silk''' : Said to have been produced in the Muromachi period, it is regarded as an important historical artifact of {{ill|lt=Hakusan worship|Q11579557|WD=Q11579557|1=Hakusan Faith}}{{Cite book|ja|author=Iwasaki Shinnosuke |title=Folk Tales and Legends of Echizen Maruoka |publisher=Iwasaki Shinnosuke |year=1979 |page=27–28 |isbn=}}. * '''Heavenly Treasure Sword''' : A treasured sword dedicated by Honda Shigeaki, lord of Maruoka. The scabbard features {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=inlay work|1=UNKNOWN}}, and the {{ill|lt=tsuba|Q1758043|WD=Q1758043|1=tsuba}} (handguard) is made of gold. The scabbard is said to be decorated with *kan-shippō*. As of 2019 (Reiwa 1), it is preserved as a treasure of Kunigami Shrine. * '''Senju Kannon of Jambudvīpa Gold''' : In {{ill|lt=862|Q24993|WD=Q24993|1=862}} (Jōgan 4), it was brought back from {{ill|lt=Tang China|Q9683|WD=Q9683|1=Tang dynasty}} by the monk Shinnyo of Settsu and is housed in a {{ill|lt=zushi|Q19993032|WD=Q19993032|1=Zushi}} made of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=ebony|1=UNKNOWN}}. Seven ring-like ornaments hang from the halo of the Kannon, with their bases made of gold, silver, and {{ill|lt=shakudō|Q11635994|WD=Q11635994|1=shakudō}}, inlaid with red, blue, and white jewels. The zushi is carved with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lotus flowers|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its curtain is adorned with beautiful gold and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lapis lazuli|1=UNKNOWN}}. ===Footnotes=== {{Reflist}} ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Kunigami Shrine}} * [http://www.kunigamijinja.jp/ Kunigami Shrine] – Official website == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kunigami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kunigami Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echizen Province]] [[Category:坂井市の建築物]] [[Category:坂井市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:福井県の旧県社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echizen Province]] [[Category:丸岡藩]] [[Category:Sankei Mandalas]]

Seven ring-like ornaments hang from the halo of the Kannon, with their bases made of gold, silver, and {{ill|lt=shakudō|Q11635994|WD=Q11635994|1=shakudō}}, inlaid with red, blue, and white jewels. The zushi is carved with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lotus flowers|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its curtain is adorned with beautiful gold and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lapis lazuli|1=UNKNOWN}}. ===Footnotes=== {{Reflist}} ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Kunigami Shrine}} * [http://www.kunigamijinja.jp/ Kunigami Shrine] – Official website == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kunigami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kunigami Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echizen Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakai]] [[Category:坂井市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:福井県の旧県社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echizen Province]] [[Category:丸岡藩]] [[Category:Sankei Mandalas]]

|Name = Kunigami Shrine |Image = [[File:Kunigami Shrine, Maruoka.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879|1=Fukui Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakai City|Q990342|WD=Q990342|1=Sakai, Fukui}}{{ill|lt=Maruoka Town|Q177273|WD=Q177273|1=Maruoka, Fukui}} Ishikido-machi 1-2 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Prince Maruko |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = Before 1200 |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Annual festivals = {{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}}, {{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963|1=October 16}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukui|label=Kunigami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kunigami Shrine''' (くにがみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Maruoka Town, {{ill|lt=Sakai City|Q990342|WD=Q990342|1=Sakai, Fukui}}, {{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|WD=Q133879|1=Fukui Prefecture}}. Its former shrine rank was prefectural shrine. It enshrines Prince Maruko, a son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|WD=Q349277|1=Emperor Keitai}}. ===History=== Listed as “Kunigami Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Sakai District|Q7402764|WD=Q7402764|1=Sakai District, Fukui}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} founded before 1200Information board within the shrine grounds. According to the shrine tradition recorded in the *Shrine Register*, the shrine originated when the placenta of Prince Maruko, the second son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|WD=Q349277|1=Emperor Keitai}} and Yahime, was buried at the place where the prince was born, called “Maruoko” (磨留古乎加), and a Shinmei shrine was established there[http://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20151027_104515 Fukui Prefecture Shrine Association]. In {{ill|lt=1576|Q6700|WD=Q6700|1=1576}} (Tenshō 4), when {{ill|lt=Shibata Katsutoyo|Q865901|WD=Q865901|1=Shibata Katsutoyo}} built {{ill|lt=Maruoka Castle|Q1143892|WD=Q1143892|1=Maruoka Castle}} at “Maruoko,” the shrine was relocated from its original site to its present location. Thereafter, it received protection and donations from successive lords of Maruoka Castle. On November 18, {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} (Meiji 7), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and on April 26, {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}}, it was specified as a shrine to receive offerings of sacred coins and ritual sticks. The {{ill|lt=1948|Q5165|WD=Q5165|1=1948}} (Shōwa 23) {{ill|lt=Fukui earthquake|Q4389555|WD=Q4389555|lt=Great Fukui Earthquake|1=1948 Fukui earthquake}} on June 28 caused the shrine buildings and trees to collapse and burn, but on July 15, {{ill|lt=1962|Q2764|WD=Q2764|1=1962}}, the main hall (Shinmei-zukuri), offering hall, and worship hall were rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1982|Q2436|WD=Q2436|1=1982}}, as a commemorative project marking the 20th anniversary of reconstruction, a treasure hall was newly built and a sacred fence erected. ===Cultural Properties=== Among its many cultural assets are the “Hanging Scroll of the Hakusan Pilgrimage Mandala, color on silk,” dating back to the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} (designated a cultural property of Fukui Prefecture{{Cite web|ja|author= |year= |url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/kaiga/itiran.html |title=Fukui Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties (Hanging Scroll of the Hakusan Pilgrimage Mandala, color on silk) |publisher=Fukui Prefecture |accessdate=2018-02-16}}), as well as the “Heavenly Treasure Sword,” said to have been dedicated by {{ill|lt=Honda Shigeaki|Q10517896|WD=Q10517896|1=Honda Shigeaki}}, lord of Maruoka Castle, and the Senju Kannon of Jambudvīpa gold. Within the shrine grounds is also a stone lantern dedicated by {{ill|lt=Arima Michizumi|Q10517531|WD=Q10517531|1=Arima Michizumi}}, the final lord of the {{ill|lt=Maruoka Domain|Q10863481|WD=Q10863481|1=Maruoka Domain}} who also served as a shogunate {{ill|lt=Rōjū|Q1196812|WD=Q1196812|1=Rōjū}}. ===Treasures=== * '''Hanging Scroll of Hakusan Toyohara Mandala, color on silk''' : Said to have been produced in the Muromachi period, it is regarded as an important historical artifact of {{ill|lt=Hakusan worship|Q11579557|WD=Q11579557|1=Hakusan Faith}}{{Cite book|ja|author=Iwasaki Shinnosuke |title=Folk Tales and Legends of Echizen Maruoka |publisher=Iwasaki Shinnosuke |year=1979 |page=27–28 |isbn=}}. * '''Heavenly Treasure Sword''' : A treasured sword dedicated by Honda Shigeaki, lord of Maruoka. The scabbard features {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=inlay work|1=UNKNOWN}}, and the {{ill|lt=tsuba|Q1758043|WD=Q1758043|1=tsuba}} (handguard) is made of gold. The scabbard is said to be decorated with *kan-shippō*. As of 2019 (Reiwa 1), it is preserved as a treasure of Kunigami Shrine. * '''Senju Kannon of Jambudvīpa Gold''' : In {{ill|lt=862|Q24993|WD=Q24993|1=862}} (Jōgan 4), it was brought back from {{ill|lt=Tang China|Q9683|WD=Q9683|1=Tang dynasty}} by the monk Shinnyo of Settsu and is housed in a {{ill|lt=zushi|Q19993032|WD=Q19993032|1=Zushi}} made of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=ebony|1=UNKNOWN}}. Seven ring-like ornaments hang from the halo of the Kannon, with their bases made of gold, silver, and {{ill|lt=shakudō|Q11635994|WD=Q11635994|1=shakudō}}, inlaid with red, blue, and white jewels. The zushi is carved with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lotus flowers|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its curtain is adorned with beautiful gold and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lapis lazuli|1=UNKNOWN}}. ===Footnotes=== {{Reflist}} ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Kunigami Shrine}} * [http://www.kunigamijinja.jp/ Kunigami Shrine] – Official website == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kunigami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kunigami Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echizen Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakai]] [[Category:History of Sakai]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:福井県の旧県社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echizen Province]] [[Category:丸岡藩]] [[Category:Sankei Mandalas]]

The {{ill|lt=1948|Q5165|qid=Q5165|1=1948}} (Shōwa 23) {{ill|lt=Fukui earthquake|Q4389555|qid=Q4389555|lt=Great Fukui Earthquake|1=1948 Fukui earthquake}} on June 28 caused the shrine buildings and trees to collapse and burn, but on July 15, {{ill|lt=1962|Q2764|qid=Q2764|1=1962}}, the main hall (Shinmei-zukuri), offering hall, and worship hall were rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1982|Q2436|qid=Q2436|1=1982}}, as a commemorative project marking the 20th anniversary of reconstruction, a treasure hall was newly built and a sacred fence erected. ===Cultural Properties=== Among its many cultural assets are the “Hanging Scroll of the Hakusan Pilgrimage Mandala, color on silk,” dating back to the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|qid=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} (designated a cultural property of Fukui Prefecture{{Cite web|ja|author= |year= |url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/kaiga/itiran.html |title=Fukui Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties (Hanging Scroll of the Hakusan Pilgrimage Mandala, color on silk) |publisher=Fukui Prefecture |accessdate=2018-02-16}}), as well as the “Heavenly Treasure Sword,” said to have been dedicated by {{ill|lt=Honda Shigeaki|Q10517896|qid=Q10517896|1=Honda Shigeaki}}, lord of Maruoka Castle, and the Senju Kannon of Jambudvīpa gold. Within the shrine grounds is also a stone lantern dedicated by {{ill|lt=Arima Michizumi|Q10517531|qid=Q10517531|1=Arima Michizumi}}, the final lord of the {{ill|lt=Maruoka Domain|Q10863481|qid=Q10863481|1=Maruoka Domain}} who also served as a shogunate {{ill|lt=Rōjū|Q1196812|qid=Q1196812|1=Rōjū}}. ===Treasures=== * '''Hanging Scroll of Hakusan Toyohara Mandala, color on silk''' : Said to have been produced in the Muromachi period, it is regarded as an important historical artifact of {{ill|lt=Hakusan worship|Q11579557|qid=Q11579557|1=Hakusan Faith}}{{Cite book|ja|author=Iwasaki Shinnosuke |title=Folk Tales and Legends of Echizen Maruoka |publisher=Iwasaki Shinnosuke |year=1979 |page=27–28 |isbn=}}. * '''Heavenly Treasure Sword''' : A treasured sword dedicated by Honda Shigeaki, lord of Maruoka. The scabbard features {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=inlay work|1=UNKNOWN}}, and the {{ill|lt=tsuba|Q1758043|qid=Q1758043|1=tsuba}} (handguard) is made of gold. The scabbard is said to be decorated with *kan-shippō*. As of 2019 (Reiwa 1), it is preserved as a treasure of Kunigami Shrine. * '''Senju Kannon of Jambudvīpa Gold''' : In {{ill|lt=862|Q24993|qid=Q24993|1=862}} (Jōgan 4), it was brought back from {{ill|lt=Tang China|Q9683|qid=Q9683|1=Tang dynasty}} by the monk Shinnyo of Settsu and is housed in a {{ill|lt=zushi|Q19993032|qid=Q19993032|1=Zushi}} made of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=ebony|1=UNKNOWN}}. Seven ring-like ornaments hang from the halo of the Kannon, with their bases made of gold, silver, and {{ill|lt=shakudō|Q11635994|qid=Q11635994|1=shakudō}}, inlaid with red, blue, and white jewels. The zushi is carved with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lotus flowers|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its curtain is adorned with beautiful gold and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lapis lazuli|1=UNKNOWN}}. ===Footnotes=== {{Reflist}} ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Kunigami Shrine}} * [http://www.kunigamijinja.jp/ Kunigami Shrine] – Official website == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kunigami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kunigami Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echizen Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakai]] [[Category:History of Sakai]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:福井県の旧県社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echizen Province]] [[Category:丸岡藩]] [[Category:Sankei Mandalas]]

{{Shinto shrine |Name = Kunigami Shrine |Image = [[File:Kunigami Shrine, Maruoka.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|qid=Q133879|1=Fukui Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakai City|Q990342|qid=Q990342|1=Sakai, Fukui}}{{ill|lt=Maruoka Town|Q177273|qid=Q177273|1=Maruoka, Fukui}} Ishikido-machi 1-2 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Prince Maruko |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = Before 1200 |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Alternate name = |Annual festivals = {{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|qid=Q2519|1=April 16}}, {{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|qid=Q2963|1=October 16}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukui|label=Kunigami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kunigami Shrine''' (くにがみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Maruoka Town, {{ill|lt=Sakai City|Q990342|qid=Q990342|1=Sakai, Fukui}}, {{ill|lt=Fukui Prefecture|Q133879|qid=Q133879|1=Fukui Prefecture}}. Its former shrine rank was prefectural shrine. It enshrines Prince Maruko, a son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|qid=Q349277|1=Emperor Keitai}}. ===History=== Listed as “Kunigami Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Sakai District|Q7402764|qid=Q7402764|1=Sakai District, Fukui}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} founded before 1200Information board within the shrine grounds. According to the shrine tradition recorded in the *Shrine Register*, the shrine originated when the placenta of Prince Maruko, the second son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|qid=Q349277|1=Emperor Keitai}} and Yahime, was buried at the place where the prince was born, called “Maruoko” (磨留古乎加), and a Shinmei shrine was established there[http://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20151027_104515 Fukui Prefecture Shrine Association]. In {{ill|lt=1576|Q6700|qid=Q6700|1=1576}} (Tenshō 4), when {{ill|lt=Shibata Katsutoyo|Q865901|qid=Q865901|1=Shibata Katsutoyo}} built {{ill|lt=Maruoka Castle|Q1143892|qid=Q1143892|1=Maruoka Castle}} at “Maruoko,” the shrine was relocated from its original site to its present location. Thereafter, it received protection and donations from successive lords of Maruoka Castle. On November 18, {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|qid=Q7761|1=1874}} (Meiji 7), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and on April 26, {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|qid=Q2056|1=1908}}, it was specified as a shrine to receive offerings of sacred coins and ritual sticks. The {{ill|lt=1948|Q5165|qid=Q5165|1=1948}} (Shōwa 23) {{ill|lt=Fukui earthquake|Q4389555|qid=Q4389555|lt=Great Fukui Earthquake|1=1948 Fukui earthquake}} on June 28 caused the shrine buildings and trees to collapse and burn, but on July 15, {{ill|lt=1962|Q2764|qid=Q2764|1=1962}}, the main hall (Shinmei-zukuri), offering hall, and worship hall were rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1982|Q2436|qid=Q2436|1=1982}}, as a commemorative project marking the 20th anniversary of reconstruction, a treasure hall was newly built and a sacred fence erected. ===Cultural Properties=== Among its many cultural assets are the “Hanging Scroll of the Hakusan Pilgrimage Mandala, color on silk,” dating back to the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|qid=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} (designated a cultural property of Fukui Prefecture{{Cite web|ja|author= |year= |url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/kaiga/itiran.html |title=Fukui Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties (Hanging Scroll of the Hakusan Pilgrimage Mandala, color on silk) |publisher=Fukui Prefecture |accessdate=2018-02-16}}), as well as the “Heavenly Treasure Sword,” said to have been dedicated by {{ill|lt=Honda Shigeaki|Q10517896|qid=Q10517896|1=Honda Shigeaki}}, lord of Maruoka Castle, and the Senju Kannon of Jambudvīpa gold. Within the shrine grounds is also a stone lantern dedicated by {{ill|lt=Arima Michizumi|Q10517531|qid=Q10517531|1=Arima Michizumi}}, the final lord of the {{ill|lt=Maruoka Domain|Q10863481|qid=Q10863481|1=Maruoka Domain}} who also served as a shogunate {{ill|lt=Rōjū|Q1196812|qid=Q1196812|1=Rōjū}}. ===Treasures=== * '''Hanging Scroll of Hakusan Toyohara Mandala, color on silk''' : Said to have been produced in the Muromachi period, it is regarded as an important historical artifact of {{ill|lt=Hakusan worship|Q11579557|qid=Q11579557|1=Hakusan Faith}}{{Cite book|ja|author=Iwasaki Shinnosuke |title=Folk Tales and Legends of Echizen Maruoka |publisher=Iwasaki Shinnosuke |year=1979 |page=27–28 |isbn=}}. * '''Heavenly Treasure Sword''' : A treasured sword dedicated by Honda Shigeaki, lord of Maruoka. The scabbard features {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=inlay work|1=UNKNOWN}}, and the {{ill|lt=tsuba|Q1758043|qid=Q1758043|1=tsuba}} (handguard) is made of gold. The scabbard is said to be decorated with *kan-shippō*. As of 2019 (Reiwa 1), it is preserved as a treasure of Kunigami Shrine. * '''Senju Kannon of Jambudvīpa Gold''' : In {{ill|lt=862|Q24993|qid=Q24993|1=862}} (Jōgan 4), it was brought back from {{ill|lt=Tang China|Q9683|qid=Q9683|1=Tang dynasty}} by the monk Shinnyo of Settsu and is housed in a {{ill|lt=zushi|Q19993032|qid=Q19993032|1=Zushi}} made of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=ebony|1=UNKNOWN}}. Seven ring-like ornaments hang from the halo of the Kannon, with their bases made of gold, silver, and {{ill|lt=shakudō|Q11635994|qid=Q11635994|1=shakudō}}, inlaid with red, blue, and white jewels. The zushi is carved with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lotus flowers|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its curtain is adorned with beautiful gold and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lapis lazuli|1=UNKNOWN}}. ===Footnotes=== {{Reflist}} ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Kunigami Shrine}} * [http://www.kunigamijinja.jp/ Kunigami Shrine] – Official website == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kunigami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kunigami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echizen Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakai]] [[Category:History of Sakai]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:福井県の旧県社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echizen Province]] [[Category:丸岡藩]] [[Category:Sankei Mandalas]]

Its former shrine rank was prefectural shrine. It enshrines Prince Maruko, a son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|qid=Q349277|1=Emperor Keitai}}. ===History=== Listed as “Kunigami Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Sakai District|Q7402764|qid=Q7402764|1=Sakai District, Fukui}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} founded before 1200Information board within the shrine grounds. According to the shrine tradition recorded in the *Shrine Register*, the shrine originated when the placenta of Prince Maruko, the second son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|qid=Q349277|1=Emperor Keitai}} and Yahime, was buried at the place where the prince was born, called “Maruoko” (磨留古乎加), and a Shinmei shrine was established there[http://www.jinja-fukui.jp/detail/index.php?ID=20151027_104515 Fukui Prefecture Shrine Association]. In {{ill|lt=1576|Q6700|qid=Q6700|1=1576}} (Tenshō 4), when {{ill|lt=Shibata Katsutoyo|Q865901|qid=Q865901|1=Shibata Katsutoyo}} built {{ill|lt=Maruoka Castle|Q1143892|qid=Q1143892|1=Maruoka Castle}} at “Maruoko,” the shrine was relocated from its original site to its present location. Thereafter, it received protection and donations from successive lords of Maruoka Castle. On November 18, {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|qid=Q7761|1=1874}} (Meiji 7), it was designated a prefectural shrine, and on April 26, {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|qid=Q2056|1=1908}}, it was specified as a shrine to receive offerings of sacred coins and ritual sticks. The {{ill|lt=1948|Q5165|qid=Q5165|1=1948}} (Shōwa 23) {{ill|lt=Fukui earthquake|Q4389555|qid=Q4389555|lt=Great Fukui Earthquake|1=1948 Fukui earthquake}} on June 28 caused the shrine buildings and trees to collapse and burn, but on July 15, {{ill|lt=1962|Q2764|qid=Q2764|1=1962}}, the main hall (Shinmei-zukuri), offering hall, and worship hall were rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1982|Q2436|qid=Q2436|1=1982}}, as a commemorative project marking the 20th anniversary of reconstruction, a treasure hall was newly built and a sacred fence erected. ===Cultural Properties=== Among its many cultural assets are the “Hanging Scroll of the Hakusan Pilgrimage Mandala, color on silk,” dating back to the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|qid=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} (designated a cultural property of Fukui Prefecture{{Cite web|ja|author= |year= |url=http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/kaiga/itiran.html |title=Fukui Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties (Hanging Scroll of the Hakusan Pilgrimage Mandala, color on silk) |publisher=Fukui Prefecture |accessdate=2018-02-16}}), as well as the “Heavenly Treasure Sword,” said to have been dedicated by {{ill|lt=Honda Shigeaki|Q10517896|qid=Q10517896|1=Honda Shigeaki}}, lord of Maruoka Castle, and the Senju Kannon of Jambudvīpa gold. Within the shrine grounds is also a stone lantern dedicated by {{ill|lt=Arima Michizumi|Q10517531|qid=Q10517531|1=Arima Michizumi}}, the final lord of the {{ill|lt=Maruoka Domain|Q10863481|qid=Q10863481|1=Maruoka Domain}} who also served as a shogunate {{ill|lt=Rōjū|Q1196812|qid=Q1196812|1=Rōjū}}. ===Treasures=== * '''Hanging Scroll of Hakusan Toyohara Mandala, color on silk''' : Said to have been produced in the Muromachi period, it is regarded as an important historical artifact of {{ill|lt=Hakusan worship|Q11579557|qid=Q11579557|1=Hakusan Faith}}{{Cite book|ja|author=Iwasaki Shinnosuke |title=Folk Tales and Legends of Echizen Maruoka |publisher=Iwasaki Shinnosuke |year=1979 |page=27–28 |isbn=}}. * '''Heavenly Treasure Sword''' : A treasured sword dedicated by Honda Shigeaki, lord of Maruoka. The scabbard features {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=inlay work|1=UNKNOWN}}, and the {{ill|lt=tsuba|Q1758043|qid=Q1758043|1=tsuba}} (handguard) is made of gold. The scabbard is said to be decorated with *kan-shippō*. As of 2019 (Reiwa 1), it is preserved as a treasure of Kunigami Shrine. * '''Senju Kannon of Jambudvīpa Gold''' : In {{ill|lt=862|Q24993|qid=Q24993|1=862}} (Jōgan 4), it was brought back from {{ill|lt=Tang China|Q9683|qid=Q9683|1=Tang dynasty}} by the monk Shinnyo of Settsu and is housed in a {{ill|lt=zushi|Q19993032|qid=Q19993032|1=Zushi}} made of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=ebony|1=UNKNOWN}}. Seven ring-like ornaments hang from the halo of the Kannon, with their bases made of gold, silver, and {{ill|lt=shakudō|Q11635994|qid=Q11635994|1=shakudō}}, inlaid with red, blue, and white jewels. The zushi is carved with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lotus flowers|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its curtain is adorned with beautiful gold and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=lapis lazuli|1=UNKNOWN}}. ===Footnotes=== {{Reflist}} ===External links=== {{Commonscat|Kunigami Shrine}} * [http://www.kunigamijinja.jp/ Kunigami Shrine] – Official website == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kunigami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kunigami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echizen Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakai]] [[Category:History of Sakai]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:福井県の旧県社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echizen Province]] [[Category:丸岡藩]] [[Category:Sankei Mandalas]]
